channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Analyst says JD Vance remarks on Iran negotiations highlight strategic messaging around oil supply and diplomacy
item.description: A political analyst has interpreted recent comments by U.S. Vice President JD Vance as revealing underlying strategic considerations in ongoing diplomatic discussions with Iran. During remarks attribu...

item.title: Klopp emerges as leading candidate to replace Nagelsmann after Germany World Cup exit
item.description: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has reportedly become the frontrunner to take over as Germany national team coach following the country’s disappointing exit from the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Reports...

item.title: Health officials confirm West Nile virus detection in Nashville mosquitoes and urge prevention measures
item.description: Health officials in Nashville, Tennessee, have confirmed the detection of West Nile virus in a mosquito sample collected in North Nashville, marking the first positive finding of the virus in the area...

item.title: OpenAI explores proposal for U.S. government equity stake amid AI regulation discussions
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly in early discussions about a proposal that would give the Trump administration a 5% equity stake in the company, according to a Financial Times report. The idea is part of broader...

item.title: Large-scale Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv leaves multiple casualties and widespread destruction
item.description: Russia launched a large-scale aerial assault on Ukraine’s capital Kyiv in the early hours of July 2, 2026, firing hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles in what officials described as one of the mo...

item.title: One person killed and seven injured after dynamite explosion at Mwanza coffee shop
item.description: One person has been confirmed dead and seven others injured after a dynamite explosion that occurred on July 1, 2026, at a coffee shop located along Sahwa ya Juu Street in Lwanhima Ward, Nyamagana Dis...

item.title: Alex Eala advances to Wimbledon third round after comeback win over Maya Joint
item.description: Filipina tennis player Alex Eala secured a landmark victory at the 2026 Wimbledon Championships after defeating Australia’s Maya Joint in a hard-fought second-round match. Eala came from a set down to...

item.title: NATO ambassador criticized after praising U.S. fast food culture in World Cup remarks
item.description: A recent interview involving NATO Ambassador Matthew Whitaker sparked widespread online reaction after he made unusual comments about what international visitors might experience in the United States ...

item.title: PZ Cussons appoints Oghenekevwe Ogefere as Company Secretary effective June 29, 2026
item.description: PZ Cussons, a well-known manufacturer of personal healthcare and consumer goods, has officially announced the appointment of Oghenekevwe Ogefere as its new Company Secretary. The announcement was made...

item.title: Ted Cruz criticized over response to NYC energy conservation advice during heat wave
item.description: The article reports on a political exchange involving U.S. Senator Ted Cruz and New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani during a severe East Coast heat wave. Mayor Mamdani issued public guidance encouragin...

item.title: Former GOP operative warns Trump’s handling of Iran conflict could lead to further wars
item.description: A former Republican political operative, Steve Schmidt, has warned that the United States may be entering a period of increased global instability following what he described as an unsuccessful confli...

item.title: Arthur Fery Advances to Third Round as Last British Player in Wimbledon Singles
item.description: Arthur Fery has emerged as the last British competitor remaining in the Wimbledon singles draw after a series of early exits for his compatriots. The 23-year-old wildcard secured his place in the thir...

item.title: Leaked video reveals internal conflict at Michael Flynn’s nonprofit tied to MAGA and anti-trafficking work
item.description: A newly obtained video reviewed by The Bulwark reportedly captures a heated confrontation involving individuals connected to former Trump national security adviser Michael Flynn’s nonprofit organizati...

item.title: DEA to temporarily schedule kratom-derived compound 7-OH as a Schedule I substance
item.description: The U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) plans to temporarily classify 7-hydroxymitragynine (7-OH), a potent compound derived from the kratom plant, as a Schedule I controlled substance. This cl...

item.title: 1940 Photograph Shows Baby Lamb Resting Beside Sleeping Boy in Wartime England
item.description: This article highlights a vintage photograph taken in 1940 in rural England during the early period of World War II. The image depicts a tender and peaceful moment between a sleeping young boy and a b...

item.title: IQM goes public via SPAC while warning that large-scale quantum computing success remains uncertain
item.description: IQM, a Finland-based full-stack quantum computing company and spinout from Aalto University, has gone public through a SPAC merger on Nasdaq at an estimated valuation of around $1.9 billion. Despite t...

item.title: Menswear critic analyzes suit fit issues of White House official Stephen Miller at Washington event
item.description: A Raw Story report covers commentary from menswear expert Derek Guy, who analyzed the suit worn by White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller during a public appearance in Washington, D.C. The e...

item.title: Dancer nearly struck by falling stage panel during Freedom 250 rehearsal in Washington, D.C.
item.description: During rehearsals for the Freedom 250 July 4 celebration in Washington, D.C., a stage malfunction nearly caused a serious accident when a large structural panel detached and fell toward performers. Ac...

item.title: Jeanine Pirro Faces Criticism Over Felony Indictment of Former Olympian in Reflecting Pool Damage Case
item.description: U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia Jeanine Pirro is facing public criticism following the felony indictment of former Olympic canoeist David Carter Hearn, 67, in connection with alleged damage...

item.title: Economist warns of slowdown in U.S. job growth following weak June labor report
item.description: An economist has raised concerns about the state of the U.S. labor market following the release of a new Department of Labor report showing weaker-than-expected job growth in June. According to the re...
